content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real stale-state bug in the success reconciliation path. The current implementation conflates a successful "no actionable items available" `bv --robot-next` response with an actual bv failure, which preserves obsolete hint state instead of clearing it.

## Votes

- `fr-16-c308d06c`: Accept
  `capture_next_step_hint` currently deserializes `bv --robot-next` output only as `NextBeadResponse`, whose required `id` and `title` fields make a message-only success payload fail parsing. That parse error is mapped to `HintCaptureOutcome::BvFailed`, and `reconcile_success` explicitly preserves any existing persisted hint in that branch. As a result, once the last actionable bead is closed, `next_step_hint.json` can remain on disk and still reference a completed bead. Treating the message-only success payload as an explicit "no next hint" outcome and clearing/removing the persisted hint is the correct behavior. Adding a regression test for `{"message":"No actionable items available"}` is also appropriate to prevent this stale-hint path from regressing.
